a_image.alt: VHS CRT Aesthetic style mood board — Analog Media · 1980s–1990s

sections.a_description: Scan lines crawling across a dark screen, chromatic aberration bleeding red and cyan at the edges of every letter, and the warm hum of a cathode ray tube — VHS CRT aesthetic captures the analog imperfection of magnetic tape media. It's the tracking artifacts, the phosphor glow, the timestamp flicker of a paused VHS frame, turned into a deliberate visual language.

VHS CRT styling shows up on horror game landing pages, indie film festival sites, analog photography portfolios, and lo-fi music labels that embrace imperfection as identity. You'll find it on creepypasta archives, retro media blogs, found-footage film projects, and anywhere that wants to feel like a mysterious tape you found in a thrift store.

a_image_cover.alt: VHS CRT Aesthetic style use case illustration
VHS CRT Aesthetic style use case illustration
VHS CRT Aesthetic style use case illustration

sections.a_description: Simulates the look of a VHS tape played on a CRT television — scan lines, color bleeding, tracking artifacts, and phosphor glow. Dark near-black background (#0a0a0a) with a faint blue-purple tint. A pseudo-element creates horizontal scan lines using repeating-linear-gradient (2px transparent, 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)). Another pseudo-element adds a subtle color-bleed effect — offset red and cyan shadows on text. The .inner panel has no distinct background, just the scan-line overlay. Text is slightly off-white (#d4d4d4) with a faint phosphor-green or amber tint. The heading gets chromatic aberration via text-shadow with offset red (#ff0040) and cyan (#00ffff) shadows. Set in VT323 — a monospace pixel font that perfectly channels the low-resolution character display of VHS on-screen text (timestamps, tracking info). Body text in Share Tech Mono for better readability at paragraph lengths while maintaining the CRT terminal feel. Heading in VT323 at 28px, line-height 1.3. Eyebrow in Share Tech Mono at 12px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, with a red tint. Body in Share Tech Mono at 15px, line-height 1.8. Buttons have a glitchy border effect — 1px solid with slight color offset. Primary: semi-transparent white background with scan-line overlay. Secondary: transparent with dotted border. Alternatives: IBM Plex Mono (cleaner CRT feel), Fira Code (modern terminal), Source Code Pro (neutral mono).
